name: Arrowhead Stadium
nickname: The Loudest Stadium in the World
tagline: The loudest stadium on Earth turns its Sea of Red into a World Cup wall of sound.

why.title: World's Loudest
why.text: Chiefs fans hold the Guinness record for loudest sports crowd at 142.2 decibels, set in 2014 and louder than a jet engine. The steep open bowl funnels every roar straight onto the field.

why.title: Sea of Red
why.text: A continuous steeply-banked single bowl with near-unobstructed sightlines and a true wall-of-sound effect. Expect a packed atmosphere unlike anything else in the tournament.

why.title: Tailgate Capital
why.text: Acres of parking fuel KC's legendary tailgating culture, with smoked-meat aromas drifting across the lots hours before kickoff.

why.title: Marquee Knockouts
why.text: Beyond four group games, Kansas City lands a Round of 32 tie and a quarterfinal, one of the biggest stages of the whole tournament.

gettingThere: The stadium sits in the Truman Sports Complex off I-70, about 8-10 miles east of downtown Kansas City, ringed by huge surface parking lots. There's nothing walkable nearby, so plan your ride in advance.
transit: ConnectKC26: the official tournament shuttle network linking the airport, stadium, and Fan Festival, the best bet if you don't have a car
Rideshare drop-off is at the Missouri Welcome Center between Gates 1 and 2; budget $40-60 each way from downtown with game-day surge
RideKC 47-Broadway bus reaches Blue Ridge Cutoff near the gates, the cheap option but slow at 55-70 minutes total
Driving: lots open about 3.5 hours before kickoff, advance general parking runs $40-60
matchday: Get to the lots three-plus hours early to soak in the tailgate scene, then clear airport-style security and be inside 60-90 minutes before kickoff. Most KC matches are evening kickoffs, so it cools down but stays muggy.
food: Joe's Kansas City: the legendary Z-Man sandwich and pulled pork
Q39 and Jack Stack: modern BBQ plus that must-try cheesy corn

food.name: BBQ Alley
food.note: local KC pitmasters; chase the burnt ends
why: Real KC pitmasters, not stadium chains
Burnt ends straight from the smoker

food.name: Smoke Brewing Co.
food.note: deep-fried burnt ends and crispy pork belly
why: Deep-fried burnt ends you won't find elsewhere
Crispy pork belly with local craft beer

food.name: Mac Shack
food.note: BBQ burnt-end mac and cheese loaded
why: Burnt ends piled on mac and cheese
Five locations, no long bowl walk

food.name: Little Pig BBQ
food.note: burnt-end sandwich plus the wild BBQ sundae
why: Burnt-end sandwich done KC-style
The BBQ sundae is a must-try

pre.name: FIFA Fan Festival
pre.where: National WWI Museum south lawn, 2 Memorial Dr; ~8-10 mi from stadium via KC Streetcar/Union Station or ConnectKC26 shuttle
why: Free fan zone, 45-foot screen, 25k capacity
Headliners and watch parties near downtown

pre.name: Tailgate lots
pre.note: legendary NFL tailgate acres — but expect a scaled-back World Cup version (FIFA fan villages take over much of the lots)
why: Legendary NFL tailgate grounds at the gates
World Cup matchdays run a different, FIFA-managed setup

pre.name: Johnny's Tavern
pre.note: heart of P&L, packed gameday fan crowd
why: Heart of the Power & Light crowd
Packed gameday energy before kickoff

pre.name: KC Live! Block
pre.note: giant open-air watch party, thousands roaring
why: Giant open-air watch party downtown
Thousands roaring at every goal

pre.name: Kelly's Westport Inn
pre.note: classic dive, cheap beer before kickoff
why: KC's oldest building, true dive cred
Cheap beer before you head out

post.name: No Other Pub
post.note: bowling, shuffleboard, ping pong, late drinks
why: Bowling, shuffleboard and ping pong
Late drinks steps from KC Live!

post.name: Tin Roof
post.note: live bands, open-air, loud and lively
why: Live bands every night, open-air
Loud and lively post-match scene

post.name: Char Bar
why: Late-night brisket and burnt-end sandwiches
Westport patio to refuel after the match

post.name: Mosaic
post.note: moody nightclub, DJ, LED wall, VIP
why: Moody nightclub with DJ and LED wall
VIP bottle service for a big night

merch.name: FIFA Official Store (online for KC Host City)
merch.where: store.fifa.com / on-site FIFA merch points at the stadium on match days

tips: Bring your own shade and water, the open bowl has zero roof or cover in June-July heat
Go cashless, the stadium and Fan Festival are card-only
Follow the FIFA clear-bag rule: one 12x6x12 inch clear bag plus a small clutch, no backpacks and no bag storage on site
Hit the FIFA Fan Festival at the National WWI Museum or the KC Live! Block in Power & Light for watch parties
Pack a poncho, not an umbrella (often restricted), and watch the radar for afternoon thunderstorms
Lower-bowl sideline seats give the best atmosphere; the upper deck has great views but a long, steep climb
weather: Hot and humid with highs around 86F in June and 91F in July plus pop-up thunderstorms, so pack sunscreen, a hat, water, and a rain poncho.
lore: Twice broke the Guinness loudest-stadium record in 2013-2014, settling at 142.2 decibels vs the Patriots
Home of the Patrick Mahomes Chiefs dynasty and multiple recent Super Bowl titles
Open-air bowl that has hosted the Chiefs since 1972, the oldest stadium in the AFC
Slated for demolition after 2031, making the World Cup one of Arrowhead's final marquee chapters

transport.summary: Arrowhead is famously car-and-tailgate-centric with seas of parking and almost no transit, and the free downtown streetcar does NOT reach it. For the World Cup the smart workaround is the official ConnectKC26 Stadium Direct bus, $15 round-trip from the Fan Festival or four park-and-ride lots.

bus.name: ConnectKC26 Stadium Direct
bus.detail: Official express motorcoach straight to the stadium; runs from 3 hours before kickoff until 2 hours after the final whistle. Requires a ConnectKC26 pass plus a match ticket; reserve online and add to your phone wallet.

bus.name: ConnectKC26 Region Direct
bus.from: 15+ metro hubs feeding the Fan Festival
bus.detail: Connector network linking the Fan Festival to hubs across the metro and Lawrence (buses every ~20 min, 30 min to Lawrence); transfer to Stadium Direct for the final leg to Arrowhead.

points.t: switch to Stadium Direct for Arrowhead

bus.name: RideKC 47-Broadway (regular service)
bus.detail: Standard city bus reaching Blue Ridge Cutoff within a walk of the gates; the cheap fallback but slow at roughly 55-70 minutes total from downtown. ConnectKC26 is far simpler on match day.

points.t: Cutoff, then a walk to gates

shuttle.name: Mobility carts at the stadium
shuttle.detail: Fans arriving via general lots, rideshare, or park-and-ride can be picked up by complimentary mobility carts and carried to the stadium entrance.

transport.fromAirport: Kansas City International (MCI), the rebuilt single terminal, is about 27-28 miles northwest; rideshare runs roughly 35-40 min and $45-70. There is no direct transit; use rideshare or connect via ConnectKC26 from a downtown/park-and-ride hub.
transport.rideshare: Designated game-day rideshare drop-off and pickup is at the stadium (reported as Lot O for the World Cup; the long-standing NFL zone is the Missouri Welcome Center between Gates 1 and 2 on Blue Ridge Cutoff). From downtown budget about $40-60 each way with match-day surge. Confirm the exact Lot on match day.
transport.bike: ConnectKC26 park-and-ride locations have bike racks on a first-come, first-served basis; there is no bike infrastructure to the isolated stadium complex itself.
tips: The free RideKC Streetcar (River Market to Country Club Plaza) does NOT go to the stadium, so do not plan to take it the whole way.
Pre-book a ConnectKC26 Stadium Direct pass (kc2026.com/transportation) before match day; passes are first-come and add straight to your phone wallet.
If you rideshare back, walk a few lots away from the designated zone before requesting a pickup to skip the post-match crush.
ConnectKC26 runs June 11 through July 13, 2026, covering every KC match day.

parking.summary: The Truman Sports Complex is ringed by vast surface lots, but for the World Cup nearly all stadium-side parking is PREPAID ONLY and linked to your match ticket, with prices climbing sharply for the knockout rounds. Buy ahead or take the shuttle.

lots.name: Gold / Red Lots (closest)
lots.detail: Premium stadium-side lots, roughly a 5-8 minute walk to the gates; sell out first.

lots.name: Outer / general lots (incl. Lots D & E)
lots.price: From ~$125 group stage; dynamic pricing has pushed peak matches past $900
lots.detail: A 10-20 minute walk through the tailgate crowds; Lots D & E offer an easier NW-route exit post-match.

lots.name: Private off-site lots
lots.detail: Independent lots near the complex (book via DIBS/ParkMobile/JustPark); cheaper but verify distance and tailgate rules.

parking.prepaid: Yes, and effectively mandatory: official World Cup parking passes must be bought in advance, are only sold to match ticket holders, and must use the same email as your ticket purchase. Pricing is dynamic and climbs as kickoff nears — reported prices run $125 to more than $900 by date and lot type. Lots open about 4.5 hours before kickoff.
parking.accessible: Accessible parking is available to guests with a valid ADA permit on a first-come, first-served basis. Fans parking in general lots, using rideshare, or arriving by park-and-ride can also be picked up by mobility carts and taken to the stadium entrance. Note the steep bowl limits ADA seating in the highest sections, so request lower-level seats.
parking.tailgating: Among the best tailgating in American sport. NFL rules permit charcoal and L.P. gas grills (no wood/fire pits), with your total setup limited to 8 feet behind your parking space; no commercial setups or selling, keep emergency lanes (two yellow lines) clear, and tailgating must stop once the match begins. FIFA may impose tighter match-day rules than NFL games, so confirm the official World Cup policy closer to match day.
tips: Buy your parking pass the moment you have match tickets, using the same email; inventory fills fast and drive-up is not the World Cup norm.
Lots open ~4.5 hours pre-kickoff; arrive early for the legendary BBQ tailgate scene.
Bring your own shade and water, the lots are open asphalt with zero cover in June-July heat.
Consider a park-and-ride + $15 shuttle instead of paying $125-$900 to park on site.
